decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently high but has room to grow within bounds. Increasing conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from ~476 to ~714 W/mK) will significantly improve lateral heat spreading and reduce the hotspot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat diffusion away from the chip, directly lowering the peak temperature. A 1.5x step is within the allowed ratio and targets the primary violation.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes extremely high relative to other materials
Material property may approach physical limits of realistic high-conductivity composites
